zone_6b_notes: Marginally hardy in Zone 6b — standard rosemary reliably overwinters only to Zone 7. In Farmington, plant in the warmest, most sheltered microclimate (south-facing wall, well-drained raised bed) and mulch heavily after ground freezes. 'Arp' is the standard cold-hardy cultivar rated to Zone 6 (possibly lower); 'Hardy Hill' is another Zone 6 option. Expect some die-back most winters; plant is often treated as an annual or overwintered in a container indoors. Harvest sprigs throughout growing season — never take more than 1/3 of growth at once.
type: tender perennial (Zone 7+); treat as annual in 6b without protection

planting.best_planting_time: Transplant late May; overwinter container indoors as insurance

water: drought tolerant once established — excellent drainage essential; clay soil is fatal
